name: spatial-containment
description: Study 2 — the autopoiesis of containment on a 1-D lattice. Interior content DIFFUSES and would ddisperse into the medium; the self-produced membrane GATES the cross-boundary flux (permeability falls as the membrane grows), so the individual is held together against diffusion. The membrane is built by the metabolism it contains, and decays when starved — containment is precarious, the spatial edition. ``membrane_on=false`` is the discriminating no-membrane negative control; ``fed=false`` starves it.

fed.description: Metabolism runs (produces interior content + grows the membrane). false starves it.

membrane_on.description: The self-produced membrane gates the boundary flux. false = the no-membrane control.
